heading: Atlas Time Series Collection
body:
To write processed data to an Atlas time series collection,
use the `$emit` pipeline stage with the following prototype form:

```json
{
  "$emit": {
    "connectionName": "<registered-connection>",
    "db": "<target-db>" | <expression>,
    "coll": "<target-coll>" | <expression>,
    "timeseries": {
      <options>
    }
  }
}
```

The `$emit` stage takes a document with the following fields:

| Field          | Type                 | Necessity | Description                                                                                                 |
| -------------- | -------------------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| connectionName | string               | Required  | Name, as it appears in the Connection Registry, of the connection to ingest data from.                      |
| db             | string \| expression | Required  | Name of, or expression that resolves to the Atlas database that contains the target time series collection. |
| coll           | string \| expression | Required  | Name of, or expression that resolves to the Atlas time series collection to write to.                       |
| timeseries     | document             | Required  | Document defining the time series fields for the collection.                                                |

> **Note:** The maximum size for documents within a time series collection is 4 MB.
> To learn more, see [Time Series Collection Limitations](manual/core/timeseries/timeseries-limitations).
